summary: Updated README CI badges to match the repository's current GitHub Actions workflow names. The old build-melee and publish-pages badges were removed and replaced with a single build.yml badge labeled "build". This was a small documentation/metadata fix with no decompilation code impact.
what_changed: Removed the stale build-melee workflow badge pointing at actions/workflows/build-melee.yml.
Removed the stale publish-pages workflow badge pointing at actions/workflows/publish-pages.yml.
Added a replacement build badge pointing at actions/workflows/build.yml.
Left existing progress, Discord, and publish-packages badges unchanged.
smart_moves: Kept the badge set aligned with the actual workflow filename, preventing README status indicators from going stale or broken.
Collapsed the obsolete build/publish-pages badge lines into the currently relevant build workflow badge rather than adding more badges.
Limited the PR to one README hunk, making the metadata fix easy to review and merge quickly.
decomp_lessons: Project-facing README metadata should be maintained alongside CI workflow renames; stale badges can mislead contributors even when the build itself is healthy.
Small infrastructure/documentation fixes are useful in a decompilation project because they preserve contributor confidence in build and progress signals.
No direct matching, struct, header, or symbol decompilation lesson is evidenced by this PR.
naming_conventions: README badge labels should mirror current workflow names; here the badge label changed from "build-melee" to "build".
GitHub Actions badge URLs must use the exact workflow YAML filename, such as actions/workflows/build.yml.
No C symbol or decompilation naming convention changes are present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code matching tactics were involved; this PR only changed README badge Markdown.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that renamed or reorganized GitHub Actions workflow files around build.yml, build-melee.yml, or publish-pages.yml.
Search for other README badge maintenance PRs in the melee repository.
Check whether publish-pages.yml was removed or replaced by another workflow before this badge fix.
